title: Slow-cooker lentil bolognese
subtitle: Puy lentils with tomatoes, red wine, and soffritto
excerpt: Puy lentils slow-cooked with onion, carrot, celery, tinned tomatoes, and red wine for six to eight hours. The lentils hold their shape and absorb the sauce, producing a meatless ragu with more body than a quick stovetop version. Serve with pasta or over toast.
sourceNotes: Lentil bolognese emerged as a mainstream British recipe in the late 2010s as plant-based cooking moved into the mainstream. Puy lentils are the correct choice — they hold their shape and provide a texture closer to ground meat than red lentils, which dissolve into the sauce. The classic soffritto base of onion, carrot, and celery is the same as the meat version.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to 3 months.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe and freeze in portions.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day. The sauce deepens and the lentils absorb more liquid as it rests.

content.text: Puy lentils need no soaking and can go in raw. Unlike red lentils, which dissolve completely, Puy lentils hold their shape over six to eight hours and provide the textural body that makes this work as a pasta sauce rather than a soup.

Heat the {{olive-oil}} in a pan over medium heat. Fry the {{onion}}, {{carrot}}, and {{celery}} for 5 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{tomato-puree}}, and {{oregano-dried}} and cook for 1 minute. Add the {{red-wine}} and let it bubble for 1 minute. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{puy-lentils}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{stock-vegetable}}, {{thyme-fresh}}, {{bay-leaves}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Cook on low for 6 to 8 hours, until the lentils are tender and have absorbed the sauce. Remove the {{thyme-fresh}} sprigs and {{bay-leaves}}. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.symptom: Lentils dissolved into the sauce rather than holding their shape
items.cause: Red lentils used rather than Puy
items.fix: Puy lentils (French green lentils) are the correct choice. Red lentils dissolve completely in 6 hours and produce a different dish entirely — more of a dal than a ragu

items.cause: Lentils absorbed all the liquid and then some
items.fix: Add 100 ml hot stock and stir. The correct consistency for pasta sauce is thick but fluid — it should coat pasta, not clump around it

items.symptom: Sauce thin and watery after 6 hours
items.cause: Too much stock added, or lentils cooked less than expected
items.fix: Remove the lid and cook on high for 20 to 30 minutes to reduce. The sauce should look like bolognese, not soup

content.text: Lentil bolognese is a batch-cook staple for households reducing meat consumption without reducing flavour. The slow cooker version is richer than the stovetop version because the lentils have hours to absorb the sauce fully. Use for pasta, lasagne, or served on toast with grated cheese.
